title: Gluten-free brownies
subtitle: Fudgy, properly chocolatey, no flour
excerpt: A fudgy chocolate brownie that uses no flour at all, just dark chocolate, butter, sugar, eggs, cocoa, and a little ground almond. The texture is dense and slightly sticky in the good way, and the absence of gluten makes it naturally more fudgy than a flour-based brownie.
sourceNotes: Flourless chocolate brownies are a well-established gluten-free baking category. The method here uses ground almonds as the binding agent in place of plain flour, following the standard flourless chocolate cake approach adapted for a traybake format.

content.text: produces brownies that are denser and more intensely chocolate-flavoured than the flour version. Ground almonds contribute body without the lightening effect of wheat flour. The batter comes together quickly and the bake time is short, the risk is overbaking, which produces a cakey rather than fudgy result. The skewer should come out with moist crumbs, not clean.

Preheat the oven to 180°C conventional (160°C fan). Grease and line a 20 × 20 cm square tin with baking paper. Melt the chocolate and butter together in a heatproof bowl over a pan of barely simmering water. Remove from the heat and stir in the sugar. Beat in the eggs one at a time, then add the vanilla. Fold in the cocoa, ground almonds, and salt with a spatula until just combined.

Pour into the tin and spread to the corners. Bake for 22 to 28 minutes. The edges should be clearly set and the surface should have a thin, slightly crackled crust. A skewer through the centre should come out with moist crumbs attached, if it comes out clean, it is overbaked. Cool in the tin for 30 minutes, then refrigerate for at least 1 hour before cutting. The gluten-free brownie needs the cold to set properly; it will look underdone at room temperature but be exactly right from the fridge.
